proofs.proof_tex: Let beta >= 1 and q in (0,1]. Write the equal grid as p_j = q j / beta for j=0,...,beta. First suppose 0 < q < 1 and put x = 1/q > 1. Let L_j be the Lagrange basis polynomial on the normalized grid t_j = j/beta, j=0,...,beta: L_j(t) = product_{m != j} (t - m/beta)/(j/beta - m/beta). For any real polynomial r of degree at most beta, set R(t)=r(qt). Lagrange interpolation gives R(x)=sum_{j=0}^beta L_j(x) R(j/beta), and R(0)=r(0). Hence r(1)-r(0)=sum_{j=0}^beta (L_j(1/q)-1_{j=0}) r(qj/beta). Define w_j=L_j(1/q)-1_{j=0}. Testing the last identity on r(u)=u^ell shows sum_j w_j p_j^0=0 and sum_j w_j p_j^ell=1 for ell=1,...,beta, so w belongs to W_beta(p^eq(beta,q)) by def:unbiased-weight-set. Put y=beta/q. Since q<1, y>beta. For each j, |L_j(1/q)| = product_{m != j} |y-m|/|j-m| <= y^beta/(j!(beta-j)!). Therefore sum_j |L_j(1/q)| <= y^beta sum_j 1/(j!(beta-j)!) = y^beta 2^beta/beta! <= 2 y^beta for beta>=1. Since y^beta >= 1, sum_j |w_j| <= sum_j |L_j(1/q)| + 1 <= 3 (beta/q)^beta. By def:amplification-criterion, A_beta(p^eq(beta,q)) <= (sum_j |w_j|)^2 <= 9 (beta/q)^(2 beta). At q=1, take endpoint weights w_0=-1, w_beta=1, and w_j=0 for 1<=j<=beta-1. The constant moment is -1+1=0, and for every ell=1,...,beta the moment is -0^ell+1^ell=1; hence w belongs to W_beta(p^eq(beta,1)). Thus A_beta(p^eq(beta,1)) <= (|w_0|+|w_beta|)^2 = 4 <= 9 beta^(2 beta) = 9 (beta/1)^(2 beta). Combining the cases proves the proposition with C_eq=9.

Fix c>1, q_max in (0,1), beta>=1, an integer k>=c beta, and q in (0,q_max]. Since k>beta and k is an integer, k>=beta+1. For any p in S_{k,q}, the nodes p_0,...,p_k are distinct and k>=beta, so lem:unbiased-weight-set-nonempty gives W_beta(p) nonempty and lem:amplification-dual-norm applies. Define x_q=2/q-1 and lambda(q)=x_q+sqrt(x_q^2-1). A direct calculation gives q lambda(q)=2-q+2 sqrt(1-q)=(1+sqrt(1-q))^2=rho(q), hence lambda(q)=rho(q)/q. For the lower bound, fix any p in S_{k,q}. Let T_beta be the Chebyshev polynomial and set r_q(u)=T_beta(2u/q-1). This polynomial has degree beta and satisfies |r_q(u)|<=1 on [0,q], so max_j |r_q(p_j)|<=1. By lem:amplification-dual-norm, A_beta(p)^(1/2) >= |r_q(1)-r_q(0)| = |T_beta(x_q)-T_beta(-1)|. The lower part of lem:continuous-chebyshev-endpoint-bound yields |T_beta(x_q)-T_beta(-1)| >= T_beta(x_q)-1 >= c_0(q_max) lambda(q)^beta uniformly for q in (0,q_max]. Therefore A_beta(p) >= c_0(q_max)^2 lambda(q)^(2 beta) = C_-(q_max) (rho(q)/q)^(2 beta), with C_-(q_max)=c_0(q_max)^2. This holds for every schedule p in S_{k,q}. For the upper bound, lem:chebyshev-schedule-admissible gives p^Ch(k,q) in S_{k,q}. Let r be any real polynomial of degree at most beta such that max_{0<=j<=k} |r(p_j^Ch(k,q))| <= 1. Set R(x)=r(q(1+x)/2). Then R has degree at most beta, and because 2 p_j^Ch(k,q)/q - 1 = -cos(pi j/k), max_{0<=j<=k} |R(-cos(pi j/k))| <= 1. By lem:oversampled-chebyshev-lobatto-norming, sup_{x in [-1,1]} |R(x)| <= K(c). Applying lem:continuous-chebyshev-endpoint-bound to R/K(c) gives |r(1)-r(0)| = |R(x_q)-R(-1)| <= K(c) C_0(q_max) lambda(q)^beta. Taking the supremum over all such r and using lem:amplification-dual-norm again gives A_beta(p^Ch(k,q))^(1/2) <= K(c) C_0(q_max) lambda(q)^beta. Hence A_beta(p^Ch(k,q)) <= C_+(c,q_max) (rho(q)/q)^(2 beta) with C_+(c,q_max)=K(c)^2 C_0(q_max)^2. Since the lower bound holds for every p in S_{k,q} and p^Ch(k,q) is admissible, M_{beta,k,q}=inf_{p in S_{k,q}} A_beta(p) is trapped between positive constant multiples of (rho(q)/q)^(2 beta). Constants independent of beta do not affect the pointwise exponential base, so the base is rho(q)/q. As q downarrow 0, rho(q)=(1+sqrt(1-q))^2 tends to 4, so rho(q)/q is asymptotic to 4/q. The equal-spacing benchmark prop:equal-spacing-benchmark exhibits the beta-dependent factor beta/q, whereas the matched upper and lower bounds here identify the beta-independent pointwise minimax base rho(q)/q whenever k>=c beta with c>1. Thus the intrinsic exponent is 2 beta, while the equal-spacing beta/q factor is not the minimax base once the rollout uses more than beta+1 measurement nodes. This theorem is an approximation-theoretic minimax statement about A_beta and M_{beta,k,q}; thm:tv-envelope-design is used separately to translate this amplification minimax into the rollout TV-envelope variance statement. The closest mathematical comparators are fixed-information results: Rivlin1974 supplies the Chebyshev exterior-point extremal inequality, EhlichZeller1964 supplies the Chebyshev-Lobatto mesh norming inequality, and MicchelliRivlin1977 studies optimal recovery from prescribed information. They do not optimize the treated-fraction sampling set p in S_{k,q}, do not impose W_beta(p)-unbiasedness for the causal total treatment effect, and do not target the stochastic total-variation design-variance criterion.

added_lemmas.statement: Assume beta>=1 and k>=beta. For every schedule p in S_{k,q}, the unbiased linear weight set W_beta(p) is nonempty.
added_lemmas.proof_tex: Fix beta>=1, k>=beta, and p in S_{k,q}. By def:budgeted-schedule-class, p_0=0 and 0<p_1<...<p_k=q, so the k+1 nodes p_0,...,p_k are distinct. Let B be the (beta+1) by (k+1) moment matrix B_{ell j}=p_j^ell for ell=0,...,beta and j=0,...,k, with p_j^0=1. If alpha=(alpha_0,...,alpha_beta) satisfies alpha^T B=0, then h(u)=sum_{ell=0}^beta alpha_ell u^ell has h(p_j)=0 for all j=0,...,k. Since k+1>=beta+1 and the p_j are distinct, h has at least beta+1 distinct zeros. A nonzero real polynomial of degree at most beta cannot have beta+1 distinct zeros, so h is identically zero and alpha=0. Therefore B has full row rank beta+1, and the map w -> Bw from R^(k+1) onto R^(beta+1) is surjective. Taking the target moment vector b=(0,1,1,...,1), there exists w with Bw=b. By def:unbiased-weight-set, this w belongs to W_beta(p).

added_lemmas.statement: For a schedule p with distinct nodes and with W_beta(p) nonempty, inf_{w in W_beta(p)} sum_j |w_j| equals sup{|r(1)-r(0)| : r is a real polynomial of degree at most beta and max_j |r(p_j)|<=1}. Consequently A_beta(p) is the square of this dual norm.
added_lemmas.proof_tex: Let V be the finite-dimensional space of real polynomials of degree at most beta. Define E:V->R^(k+1) by E r=(r(p_0),...,r(p_k)) and L:V->R by L(r)=r(1)-r(0). A vector w represents L through the node evaluations exactly when L(r)=sum_j w_j r(p_j) for every r in V. Testing this identity on monomials 1,u,...,u^beta gives precisely the moment equations in def:unbiased-weight-set, so the representing vectors are exactly W_beta(p). For every w in W_beta(p) and every r with ||E r||_infty<=1, |L(r)|=|sum_j w_j r(p_j)| <= sum_j |w_j|, hence the displayed supremum is at most the displayed infimum. Conversely, because W_beta(p) is nonempty, L vanishes on ker E and therefore descends to a linear functional L_tilde on the subspace E(V) of R^(k+1). Its operator norm for the sup norm is precisely sup{|L(r)|: ||E r||_infty<=1}. In finite dimension, extend L_tilde to a linear functional on all of R^(k+1) with the same sup-norm operator norm. Such a functional has the form z -> sum_j w_j z_j, and its operator norm against ||.||_infty is sum_j |w_j|. The restriction condition says w represents L, hence w in W_beta(p), and its l1 norm equals the supremum. This gives equality. Squaring and using def:amplification-criterion gives the assertion for A_beta(p).

added_lemmas.statement: If R is a real polynomial of degree at most beta and 0 <= beta < k, then sup_{x in [-1,1]} |R(x)| <= sec(pi beta/(2k)) max_{0<=j<=k} |R(-cos(pi j/k))|.

source.locator: Main Chebyshev-Lobatto mesh inequality, Mathematische Zeitschrift 86, 41-44

added_lemmas.proof_tex: Ehlich-Zeller Chebyshev-Lobatto mesh inequality: for a real algebraic polynomial R of degree at most beta with 0 <= beta < k, the oscillation between the Chebyshev-Lobatto grid points is bounded by sup_{-1<=x<=1}|R(x)| <= sec(pi beta/(2k)) max_{0<=j<=k}|R(-cos(pi j/k))|.

added_lemmas.statement: For each c>1 there is a finite constant K(c) such that, for every beta>=1, every integer k>=c beta, and every real polynomial R of degree at most beta, sup_{x in [-1,1]} |R(x)| <= K(c) max_{0<=j<=k} |R(-cos(pi j/k))|.
added_lemmas.proof_tex: Fix c>1, beta>=1, k>=c beta, and a real polynomial R of degree at most beta. Since k>=c beta and c>1, beta<k. Applying lem:ehlich-zeller-lobatto-mesh gives sup_{x in [-1,1]} |R(x)| <= sec(pi beta/(2k)) max_{0<=j<=k} |R(-cos(pi j/k))|. The function sec(t)=1/cos(t) is increasing on [0,pi/2), and beta/k <= 1/c, so sec(pi beta/(2k)) <= sec(pi/(2c)). Therefore the claim holds with K(c)=sec(pi/(2c)), which is finite because c>1.

added_lemmas.statement: Let beta>=1, q in (0,1), x_q=2/q-1, lambda(q)=x_q+sqrt(x_q^2-1), and rho(q)=q lambda(q)=(1+sqrt(1-q))^2. For every degree-beta polynomial R with sup_{x in [-1,1]}|R(x)|<=1, |R(x_q)-R(-1)| <= C(q_max) lambda(q)^beta uniformly over q in (0,q_max]; and the Chebyshev polynomial T_beta gives the matching lower order |T_beta(x_q)-T_beta(-1)| >= T_beta(x_q)-1 >= c(q_max) lambda(q)^beta.
added_lemmas.proof_tex: First prove the Chebyshev extremal inequality. Fix x_0>1 and let P be real with deg(P)<=beta and sup_{[-1,1]} |P| <= 1. Since T_beta(x_0)>0, suppose for contradiction that |P(x_0)| > T_beta(x_0). Choose s in {-1,1} so s P(x_0)>T_beta(x_0), set a=s P(x_0)/T_beta(x_0)>1, and define H(x)=s P(x)-a T_beta(x). Let t_j=cos(j pi/beta), j=0,...,beta. Then T_beta(t_j)=(-1)^j. If j is even, H(t_j)<=1-a<0; if j is odd, H(t_j)>=-1+a>0. Thus the intermediate value theorem gives one zero of H in each open interval between consecutive t_j, for beta distinct zeros in (-1,1). Also H(x_0)=0, giving one further zero outside [-1,1]. Hence H has at least beta+1 distinct zeros although deg(H)<=beta, a contradiction. Therefore |P(x_0)|<=T_beta(x_0). Apply this with x_0=x_q. Since |R(-1)|<=1, |R(x_q)-R(-1)|<=T_beta(x_q)+1. Write x_q=cosh t with t>0. Then lambda(q)=exp(t) and T_beta(x_q)=cosh(beta t)=(lambda(q)^beta+lambda(q)^(-beta))/2. Because q<=q_max<1, lambda(q)>=lambda(q_max)>1, so T_beta(x_q)+1 <= C(q_max) lambda(q)^beta for a finite constant C(q_max). For the lower order take R=T_beta. It is bounded by one on [-1,1], and T_beta(-1)=(-1)^beta, so |T_beta(x_q)-T_beta(-1)| >= T_beta(x_q)-1. Moreover T_beta(x_q)-1 = (1/2) lambda(q)^beta (1-lambda(q)^(-beta))^2 >= (1/2)(1-lambda(q_max)^(-1))^2 lambda(q)^beta. Thus c(q_max)=(1/2)(1-lambda(q_max)^(-1))^2 is positive and proves the claimed matching lower order. Finally, q lambda(q)=q(2/q-1+sqrt((2/q-1)^2-1))=2-q+2 sqrt(1-q)=(1+sqrt(1-q))^2, giving the displayed rho(q).

added_lemmas.statement: For every integer k>=1 and q in (0,1], the shifted Chebyshev-Lobatto schedule p^Ch(k,q) belongs to S_{k,q}.
added_lemmas.proof_tex: By def:chebyshev-schedule, p_j^Ch(k,q)=q(1-cos(pi j/k))/2. At j=0 this is q(1-1)/2=0, and at j=k this is q(1-(-1))/2=q. If 0<=j<l<=k, then pi j/k<pi l/k and cosine is strictly decreasing on [0,pi], so 1-cos(pi j/k)<1-cos(pi l/k). Multiplication by q/2>0 preserves the strict inequality. Therefore 0=p_0^Ch<p_1^Ch<...<p_k^Ch=q, which is exactly membership in S_{k,q} by def:budgeted-schedule-class.

added_lemmas.statement: Assume beta>=1 and k>=beta. For every q in (0,1] and every p in S_{k,q}, inf_{w in W_beta(p)} sup_{P in P_beta} w' Gamma_P(p) w <= (sigma_0^2/n) A_beta(p). Consequently R_exact(beta,k,q) <= (sigma_0^2/n) M_{beta,k,q}.
added_lemmas.proof_tex: Fix beta>=1, k>=beta, q in (0,1], and p in S_{k,q}. By lem:unbiased-weight-set-nonempty, W_beta(p) is nonempty. Fix w in W_beta(p). The following envelope step uses only ass:round-mean-variance-envelope: for any law P satisfying Var_pi(bar_Y_j)<=sigma_0^2/n for each j, let X_j=bar_Y_j-E_pi bar_Y_j. Since Gamma_P(p) is the covariance matrix of (bar_Y_0,...,bar_Y_k), w' Gamma_P(p) w = Var_pi(sum_j w_j bar_Y_j)=||sum_j w_j X_j||_2^2. The triangle inequality in L_2(pi) gives ||sum_j w_j X_j||_2 <= sum_j |w_j| ||X_j||_2 <= (sigma_0/sqrt(n)) sum_j |w_j|. Therefore w' Gamma_P(p) w <= (sigma_0^2/n)(sum_j |w_j|)^2. In particular, by def:rollout-law-class every P in P_beta satisfies ass:round-mean-variance-envelope, so the same bound holds uniformly over the sup defining the exact risk. Taking sup_{P in P_beta}, then inf_{w in W_beta(p)}, gives inf_{w in W_beta(p)} sup_{P in P_beta} w' Gamma_P(p) w <= (sigma_0^2/n) inf_{w in W_beta(p)}(sum_j |w_j|)^2 = (sigma_0^2/n) A_beta(p) by def:amplification-criterion. Taking infimum over p in S_{k,q} and using def:exact-nested-risk and def:amplification-criterion gives R_exact(beta,k,q) <= (sigma_0^2/n) M_{beta,k,q}.

added_lemmas.statement: Fix c>1, beta>=1, k=ceil(c beta), q in (0,q_max], and rho(q)=(1+sqrt(1-q))^2. The admissible shifted Chebyshev-Lobatto schedule satisfies inf_{w in W_beta(p^Ch(k,q))} sup_{P in P_beta} w' Gamma_P(p^Ch(k,q)) w <= (sigma_0^2/n) C_+(c,q_max) (rho(q)/q)^(2 beta). Consequently R_exact(beta,k,q) <= (sigma_0^2/n) C_+(c,q_max) (rho(q)/q)^(2 beta).
added_lemmas.proof_tex: Fix c>1, beta>=1, k=ceil(c beta), and q in (0,q_max]. Since c>1, k>=beta+1 and hence k>=beta. Since q<=q_max<1, q in (0,1]. By lem:chebyshev-schedule-admissible, p^Ch(k,q) belongs to S_{k,q}. Applying lem:exact-risk-envelope-upper to this schedule gives inf_{w in W_beta(p^Ch(k,q))} sup_{P in P_beta} w' Gamma_P(p^Ch(k,q)) w <= (sigma_0^2/n) A_beta(p^Ch(k,q)). The upper half of thm:chebyshev-minimax gives A_beta(p^Ch(k,q)) <= C_+(c,q_max) (rho(q)/q)^(2 beta). Combining the displays proves the fixed-Chebyshev exact-risk upper bound. Finally, by def:exact-nested-risk, R_exact(beta,k,q) is the infimum of the same criterion over all p in S_{k,q}; since p^Ch(k,q) is admissible, R_exact(beta,k,q) is at most the displayed Chebyshev value.

open_obligations.what_is_open: Whether the shifted Chebyshev-Lobatto schedule actually attains the exact finite-population nested-rollout minimax value R_exact(beta,k,q), not only the TV-envelope rate.
open_obligations.obstruction: The frozen primitives provide the round-mean variance envelope Var_pi(bar_Y_j)<=sigma_0^2/n, which upper-bounds every exact covariance risk by the l1 amplification A_beta(p). They do not provide a converse covariance construction or a characterization of the attainable matrices Gamma_P(p) under monotone Bernoulli rollout. Without such a lower-envelope or least-favorable finite-population construction, exact optimality of p^Ch for R_exact cannot be derived from the available primitives.
open_obligations.attempted: The TV-envelope route closes completely: lem:exact-risk-envelope-upper transfers the Chebyshev upper bound for A_beta(p^Ch(k,q)) to the exact risk. Attempts to compare R_exact from below with a positive multiple of A_beta(p), or to realize the rank-one worst-case covariance used by the TV envelope inside P_beta for every schedule, require covariance attainability assumptions not present in the frozen core.
open_obligations.partial_result: lem:exact-chebyshev-rate-feasible proves that for beta>=1, k=ceil(c beta), q in (0,q_max], and rho(q)=(1+sqrt(1-q))^2, inf_{w in W_beta(p^Ch(k,q))} sup_{P in P_beta} w' Gamma_P(p^Ch(k,q)) w <= (sigma_0^2/n) C_+(c,q_max) (rho(q)/q)^(2 beta), and hence R_exact(beta,k,q) <= (sigma_0^2/n) C_+(c,q_max) (rho(q)/q)^(2 beta). Thus Chebyshev-Lobatto is exact-risk rate-feasible at the TV-envelope minimax exponent and pointwise base; exact optimizer status remains open.
